{
  "schema_version": "stella.literature_hvs_candidates.v0.1",
  "generated_at": "2026-06-13T03:54:00",
  "paper": {
    "arxiv_id": "2003.12766",
    "bibcode": "2020A&A...637A..53K",
    "title": "Hypervelocity Stars in the Gaia era. Revisiting the most extreme stars from the MMT survey",
    "month": "2020-03",
    "source_note_json": "notes/2020/2020-03/2020-03.json",
    "links": {
      "abs": "https://arxiv.org/abs/2003.12766",
      "pdf": "https://arxiv.org/pdf/2003.12766"
    }
  },
  "inputs": {
    "paper_dir": "literature/2003.12766",
    "audit_path": "literature/2003.12766/audit.json",
    "catalog_review_path": "literature/2003.12766/catalog_review.json",
    "catalog_extraction_path": "literature/2003.12766/catalog_extraction.json",
    "ecsv_paths": [
      "literature/2003.12766/catalog_tables/table-tab-atmos-results.ecsv",
      "literature/2003.12766/catalog_tables/table-tab-B1085.ecsv",
      "literature/2003.12766/catalog_tables/table-tab-hvs5.ecsv",
      "literature/2003.12766/catalog_tables/table-tab-stellar_params.ecsv",
      "literature/2003.12766/catalog_tables/table-tab-kinematic-AS.ecsv",
      "literature/2003.12766/catalog_tables/table-tab-kinematic-TF.ecsv"
    ]
  },
  "extraction": {
    "status": "candidates_found",
    "extracted_at": "2026-06-13T03:54:00",
    "extractor": "agent",
    "summary": "The paper reanalyzes 40 stars from the MMT HVS survey using new spectroscopic models and Gaia DR2 astrometry. 37 stars have full 6D kinematics and are presented as hypervelocity star candidates with bound/unbound probabilities. The extraction includes all 37 kinematic sample stars as candidates possibly unbound from the Milky Way.",
    "tooling": {
      "agent_runtime": "",
      "model_id": "",
      "prompt_version": "",
      "request_parameters": {}
    }
  },
  "method_chain": [
    {
      "id": "step-01",
      "depends_on": [],
      "step_type": "input_catalog",
      "summary": "MMT HVS survey sample of 42 B-type stars from Brown et al. (2014), restricted to the 40 stars for which MMT spectra are available. Raw spectra are provided for analysis.",
      "inputs": [],
      "outputs": ["MMT raw spectra", "object list (40 stars)"],
      "parameters": [],
      "source_refs": [
        {
          "kind": "text",
          "path": "literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex",
          "start_line": 99,
          "end_line": 99,
          "context": "we extend the spectrophotometric analysis to 40 out of the 42 high-velocity outliers of the MMT HVS sample."
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-02",
      "depends_on": [],
      "step_type": "input_catalog",
      "summary": "Multi-band photometry compiled from GALEX, Gaia DR2, SDSS DR12, Pan-STARRS1, SkyMapper DR1.1, BATC, VST-ATLAS-DR3, VST-KiDS-DR3, APASS-DR9, UKIDSS-DR9, and VISTA via VizieR.",
      "inputs": [],
      "outputs": ["multi-band photometry"],
      "parameters": [],
      "source_refs": [
        {
          "kind": "text",
          "path": "literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex",
          "start_line": 272,
          "end_line": 272,
          "context": "Photometric data were compiled from a variety of surveys covering the ultraviolet, the optical, and the infrared mainly using the VizieR catalog access tool."
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-03",
      "depends_on": [],
      "step_type": "astrometric_calibration",
      "summary": "Proper motions from Gaia DR2 and HST/WFC3+ACS. The more precise measurement is adopted per star: HST for HVS1, HVS10, HVS12, HVS13; Gaia DR2 for B711; all others use Gaia DR2. RUWE < 1.4 verified for all.",
      "inputs": ["Gaia DR2 proper motions", "HST proper motions"],
      "outputs": ["adopted proper motions (37 stars)"],
      "parameters": [],
      "source_refs": [
        {
          "kind": "text",
          "path": "literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex",
          "start_line": 288,
          "end_line": 288,
          "context": "Proper motions were mainly taken from Gaia DR2. ... we chose the more precise measurement when Gaia as well as HST proper motions were available, which implies that we used HST data for HVS1, HVS10, HVS12, and HVS13 and Gaia DR2 data for B711."
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-04",
      "depends_on": ["step-01", "step-03"],
      "step_type": "sample_selection",
      "summary": "From the 40 stars with spectra, 37 have complete astrometric data. HVS11, HVS14, HVS23 lack proper motions and are excluded. This step defines the kinematic sample of 37 stars.",
      "inputs": ["spectroscopic sample", "proper motions"],
      "outputs": ["kinematic sample (37 stars)"],
      "parameters": [],
      "source_refs": [
        {
          "kind": "text",
          "path": "literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex",
          "start_line": 284,
          "end_line": 284,
          "context": "proper motion measurements were not available for HVS11, HVS14, and HVS23, which is why the kinematic analysis encompasses only 37 out of the 40 stars in the sample."
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-05",
      "depends_on": ["step-01"],
      "step_type": "radial_velocity_measurement",
      "summary": "Radial velocities obtained from χ² minimization fitting of flux-calibrated MMT spectra against synthetic model spectra. The fit simultaneously solves for radial velocity; other parameters are fitted but their adoption belongs to step-06.",
      "inputs": ["MMT flux-calibrated spectra"],
      "outputs": ["radial_velocity"],
      "parameters": [],
      "source_refs": [
        {
          "kind": "text",
          "path": "literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex",
          "start_line": 150,
          "end_line": 150,
          "context": "four fitting parameters for the stellar spectrum: the effective temperature T_eff, the surface gravity log(g), the projected rotational velocity v sin i, and the radial velocity v_rad."
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-06",
      "depends_on": ["step-01"],
      "step_type": "stellar_parameter_inference",
      "summary": "T_eff, log g, and v sin i derived from the same spectral fitting procedure as radial velocities. The ADS grid of synthetic spectra (solar composition, 9000-16000 K, log g 3.0-4.8) is used. Results in Table tab:atmos-results.",
      "inputs": ["MMT flux-calibrated spectra", "model grid"],
      "outputs": ["T_eff", "log_g", "v_sin_i"],
      "parameters": [],
      "source_refs": [
        {
          "kind": "text",
          "path": "literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex",
          "start_line": 150,
          "end_line": 150,
          "context": "four fitting parameters ... T_eff, log(g), v sin i, and v_rad."
        },
        {
          "kind": "text",
          "path": "literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex",
          "start_line": 110,
          "end_line": 128,
          "context": "Table tab:atmos-results listing spectroscopic results."
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-07",
      "depends_on": ["step-02", "step-06"],
      "step_type": "distance_estimation",
      "summary": "Spectrophotometric distances from SED fitting. Observed SEDs from compiled photometry are fit with synthetic SEDs (Atlas12 models, fixed T_eff and log g) to obtain angular diameter Θ and E(B-V). Combined with radius from step-08 to compute distance d = 2R/Θ.",
      "inputs": ["multi-band photometry", "T_eff", "log_g", "stellar radius"],
      "outputs": ["distance", "angular_diameter", "E_BminusV"],
      "parameters": [
        {
          "name": "other",
          "raw_value": "R_V = 3.1",
          "value": "3.1",
          "source_refs": [
            {
              "kind": "text",
              "path": "literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex",
              "start_line": 276,
              "end_line": 276,
              "context": "R_V fixed at 3.1, its typical value for the interstellar medium."
            }
          ]
        }
      ],
      "source_refs": [
        {
          "kind": "text",
          "path": "literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex",
          "start_line": 276,
          "end_line": 280,
          "context": "The resulting observed SEDs were compared to synthetic ones ... The angular diameters Θ were combined with the stellar radii R to calculate the spectrophotometric distances d."
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-08",
      "depends_on": ["step-06"],
      "step_type": "stellar_parameter_inference",
      "summary": "Derivation of stellar masses, ages, radii, luminosities, and Ω/Ω_crit from position in Kiel diagram compared to rotating MS evolutionary tracks (Georgy et al. 2013). Stellar radii feed into distance estimation.",
      "inputs": ["T_eff", "log_g", "evolutionary tracks"],
      "outputs": ["mass", "age", "radius", "luminosity", "Omega_over_Omega_crit"],
      "parameters": [],
      "source_refs": [
        {
          "kind": "text",
          "path": "literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex",
          "start_line": 226,
          "end_line": 226,
          "context": "stellar masses M, ages τ, radii R, luminosities L, and ratios ... Ω/Ω_crit can be derived by comparing the stars' position in the Kiel diagram with theoretical predictions."
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-09",
      "depends_on": [],
      "step_type": "solar_position_and_motion",
      "summary": "Solar position and velocity assumptions for Galactocentric coordinate transformation. Paper states R0 = 8.3 kpc, Sun on negative x-axis. Other parameters (z0, v_circ, solar peculiar motion) are adopted from Irrgang et al. (2013) / Paper II but not explicitly reported here.",
      "inputs": [],
      "outputs": ["solar Galactocentric position and velocity"],
      "parameters": [
        {
          "name": "R0",
          "raw_value": "8.3",
          "value": "8.3",
          "unit": "kpc",
          "source_refs": [
            {
              "kind": "text",
              "path": "literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex",
              "start_line": 325,
              "end_line": 325,
              "context": "the solar radius (dashed; 8.3 kpc)"
            }
          ]
        },
        {
          "name": "z0",
          "raw_value": "not reported",
          "value": "",
          "description": "Not explicitly stated in this paper; adopted from Irrgang et al. (2013) via Paper II.",
          "source_refs": []
        },
        {
          "name": "v_circ_sun",
          "raw_value": "not reported",
          "value": "",
          "description": "Not explicitly stated in this paper.",
          "source_refs": []
        },
        {
          "name": "solar_motion_u",
          "raw_value": "not reported",
          "value": "",
          "description": "Not explicitly stated in this paper.",
          "source_refs": []
        },
        {
          "name": "solar_motion_v",
          "raw_value": "not reported",
          "value": "",
          "description": "Not explicitly stated in this paper.",
          "source_refs": []
        },
        {
          "name": "solar_motion_w",
          "raw_value": "not reported",
          "value": "",
          "description": "Not explicitly stated in this paper.",
          "source_refs": []
        }
      ],
      "source_refs": [
        {
          "kind": "text",
          "path": "literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex",
          "start_line": 307,
          "end_line": 307,
          "context": "right-handed Cartesian Galactic coordinate system in which the Sun is located on the negative x-axis and the z-axis points to the Galactic north pole."
        },
        {
          "kind": "text",
          "path": "literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex",
          "start_line": 284,
          "end_line": 284,
          "context": "The strategy of the kinematic analysis is identical to that of Paper II."
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-10",
      "depends_on": ["step-03", "step-05", "step-07", "step-09"],
      "step_type": "velocity_calculation",
      "summary": "Construction of full 6D phase space: positions, proper motions, radial velocities, and distances combined to compute Galactocentric Cartesian positions (x,y,z) and velocities (vx,vy,vz). Galactic rest-frame velocity v_Grf = (vx²+vy²+vz²)^(1/2).",
      "inputs": ["RA,Dec", "proper motions", "radial velocities", "distances", "solar parameters"],
      "outputs": ["galactocentric_x", "galactocentric_y", "galactocentric_z", "galactocentric_vx", "galactocentric_vy", "galactocentric_vz", "v_Grf"],
      "parameters": [],
      "source_refs": [
        {
          "kind": "text",
          "path": "literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex",
          "start_line": 284,
          "end_line": 284,
          "context": "Complementing our revised radial velocities and spectrophotometric distances with positions and proper motions ... gave us the full six-dimensional phase space information."
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-11",
      "depends_on": [],
      "step_type": "galactic_potential_model",
      "summary": "Two Galactic mass models: Model I (revision of Allen & Santillan 1991) and Model II (Wilkinson & Evans 1999 flat rotation curve). Both calibrated with Gaia DR2 constraints. Used to compute local escape velocities and integrate orbits.",
      "inputs": [],
      "outputs": ["potential_models", "escape_velocity"],
      "parameters": [
        {
          "name": "potential_name",
          "raw_value": "Model I (Allen & Santillan revision)",
          "value": "Model I (Allen & Santillan revision)",
          "source_refs": [
            {
              "kind": "text",
              "path": "literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex",
              "start_line": 293,
              "end_line": 293,
              "context": "Model~I is a revision of the popular model by Allen & Santillan (1991)"
            }
          ]
        },
        {
          "name": "potential_name",
          "raw_value": "Model II (Wilkinson & Evans flat rotation)",
          "value": "Model II (Wilkinson & Evans flat rotation)",
          "source_refs": [
            {
              "kind": "text",
              "path": "literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex",
              "start_line": 293,
              "end_line": 293,
              "context": "Model~II is based on the flat rotation curve model by Wilkinson & Evans (1999)"
            }
          ]
        }
      ],
      "source_refs": [
        {
          "kind": "text",
          "path": "literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex",
          "start_line": 291,
          "end_line": 293,
          "context": "The trajectories of all targets were computed in two different Milky Way mass models."
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-12",
      "depends_on": ["step-10", "step-11", "step-08"],
      "step_type": "orbit_integration",
      "summary": "Monte Carlo orbit integration with 1.5 million realizations, backwards in time up to 15 Gyr. Correlated proper motion errors from Gaia DR2 included. Plane-crossing events within 99% confidence limit on stellar age are recorded. Flight times and disk-crossing parameters derived.",
      "inputs": ["6D phase space", "potential models", "stellar ages"],
      "outputs": ["orbit_ensembles", "plane_crossing_positions", "plane_crossing_velocities", "flight_time"],
      "parameters": [],
      "source_refs": [
        {
          "kind": "text",
          "path": "literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex",
          "start_line": 284,
          "end_line": 284,
          "context": "Propagation of uncertainties ... standard Monte Carlo approach with 1.5 million realizations."
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-13",
      "depends_on": ["step-12", "step-11"],
      "step_type": "escape_or_bound_assessment",
      "summary": "Bound/unbound assessment: local escape velocity v_esc from potential model, compared to v_Grf. Bound probability P_b (%) from fraction of bound Monte Carlo realizations. Values tabulated for both Model I and Model II.",
      "inputs": ["v_Grf", "potential model"],
      "outputs": ["v_esc", "v_Grf_minus_v_esc", "P_b"],
      "parameters": [],
      "source_refs": [
        {
          "kind": "text",
          "path": "literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex",
          "start_line": 307,
          "end_line": 307,
          "context": "The Galactic rest-frame velocity v_Grf, the local Galactic escape velocity v_esc ... are listed."
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-14",
      "depends_on": ["step-12", "step-08"],
      "step_type": "origin_assessment",
      "summary": "Classification of stars into origin categories: unconstrained, outer rim, disk origin. Ejection velocities v_ej,p computed at plane crossing, correcting for Galactic rotation. Flight times compared to stellar ages.",
      "inputs": ["plane_crossing_locations", "stellar ages", "plane_crossing_velocities"],
      "outputs": ["origin_category", "ejection_velocity", "origin_site"],
      "parameters": [],
      "source_refs": [
        {
          "kind": "text",
          "path": "literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex",
          "start_line": 329,
          "end_line": 331,
          "context": "we group the stars in three categories based on their inferred spatial origin."
        },
        {
          "kind": "text",
          "path": "literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex",
          "start_line": 302,
          "end_line": 302,
          "context": "the ejection velocity v_ej,p ... is a more useful quantity."
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-15",
      "depends_on": ["step-01"],
      "step_type": "quality_filter",
      "summary": "Quality assessment of MMT spectral data: wavelength-averaged S/N, reduced χ² at best fit, number of individual exposures per star. These metrics appear in Table tab:atmos-results as quality indicators. RUWE from Gaia DR2 used for astrometric quality (all <1.4).",
      "inputs": ["MMT spectra", "Gaia DR2 RUWE"],
      "outputs": ["S_N", "reduced_chi2", "N_exposures", "RUWE"],
      "parameters": [],
      "source_refs": [
        {
          "kind": "text",
          "path": "literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex",
          "start_line": 144,
          "end_line": 144,
          "context": "the average S/N of the co-added spectra is only of the order of 10--30."
        },
        {
          "kind": "text",
          "path": "literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex",
          "start_line": 110,
          "end_line": 128,
          "context": "Table tab:atmos-results listing S/N_tot, χ²_red, and N."
        },
        {
          "kind": "text",
          "path": "literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex",
          "start_line": 288,
          "end_line": 288,
          "context": "For all objects in the sample, RUWE is below the recommended value of 1.4."
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
